hadiths.chapter: 26. CHAPTER ON JUDGING BY ADMINISTERING AN OATH
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Yasin Al-Dareer, from Abdul Rahman ibn Abi Abdullah, who 
said: I asked the Sheikh, meaning Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al -Kadhim {a.s}, "Inform me 
about a man who claims a right against another man but has no evidence to prove his 
claim."  
Imam {a.s} said: "The defendant must take an oath. If he swears, then the claimant has 
no right. However, if the defendant refers the oath back to the claimant and the 
claimant refuses to swear, then he has no right either. 
If the person against whom the claim is made has died and evidence is presented 
against him, the claimant must take an oath by Allah {SWT}, there is no God but He {SWT}, 
that the deceased owed him the claimed right. If he swears, the claim is valid; 
otherwise, he has no right. 
This is because we do not know  - perhaps the deceased settled the debt through 
evidence unknown to us or without evidence before his death.  
Therefore, an oath along with the evidence is required. If the claimant makes a claim 
without evidence, he has no right because the defendant is not alive. Had the 
defendant been alive, he would have been required to either take an oath, 
acknowledge the cl aim, or refer the oath back. Hence, no right is established for the 
claimant." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.63

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 26, H. 3343
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON JUDGING BY ADMINISTERING AN OATH

hadiths.chapter: 27. CHAPTER ON THE JUDGMENT REGARDING TWO
hadiths.ar:
3345  -  ق َّالَّ إ َّن ُّو ن َّصِي زٍ : وَّ سَّا َّلْث ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي َّا ْي ِى  إَّلْق َّوْمَّ ق َّي َّذ َّعِى  ذ إرإً ق ِى  إ َّي ْذِي هِمْ وَّ ي ُّق ِي مُّ 
إَّلْن َّي ِّن َّه َّ وَّ ي ُّق ِي مُّ إَّل َّذ ِى  ق ِى  ي َّذِةِ إَّلذ َّإرُّ إَّلْن َّي ِّن َّه َّ إ َّن  َّهَّا وَّرِي َّهَّا عَّن ْ إ َّن ِي هِ وَّ لأَّ ي َّذْرِى  كَّي ْف َّ إ َّ مْزُّهَّا ق َّق َّال 
وَّ ي ُّذْق َّغُّ إ ِلَّي ْهِ  
ق َّالَّ مُّصَّن ِّف ُّ هَّذ َّإ إلْكِي َّاب ِ رَّجِمَّهُّ  إلل َّهُّ لَّوْ ق َّالَّ إل َّذ ِى  ق ِى  ي َّذِةِ إلذ َّإرُّ إ َّن  َّهَّا لِى  وَّ هِى َّ مِلْكِى  وَّ إ َّق َّامَّ عَّلَّى ذ َّلِك َّ ن َّي ِّن َّه ً  وَّ إ َّق َّامَّ إلْمُّذ َّعِى  عَّلَّى  
ذَّعْوَّإةُّ ن َّي ِّن َّه ً كَّان َّ إلْحَّق  ُّ إ َّن ْ ي ُّحْكَّمَّ ن ِهَّا لِلْمُّذ َّعِى  لِأ َّن  َّ إلل َّهَّ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ إ ِن  َّمَّا إ َّوْج َّث َّ  إلْن َّي ِّن َّه َّ عَّلَّى إلْمُّذ َّعِى  وَّ لَّمْ ي ُّوج ِن ْهَّا عَّلَّى إلْمُّذ َّعَّى 
عَّلَّي ْهِ  وَّ لَّكِن  َّ هَّذ َّإ إلْمُّذ َّعَّى عَّلَّي ْهِ ذ َّكَّزَّ إ َّن  َّهُّ وَّرِي َّهَّا عَّن ْ إ َّن ِي هِ وَّ لَّأ ي َّذْرِى  كَّي ْف َّ إ َّمْزُّهَّا ق َّلِهَّذ َّإ إ َّوْج َّث َّ  إلْحُّكْمَّ ن ِاسْي ِحْلَّاف ِ إ َّكْي َّزِهِمْ ن َّي ِّن َّه ً وَّ 
ذَّق ْغِ إلذ َّإرِ إ ِلَّي ْهِ وَّ لَّوْ إ َّن  َّ رَّج ُّلًا إذ َّعَّى عَّلَّى رَّج ُّلٍ عَّق َّارإً إ َّوْ جَّي َّوَّإن اً إ َّوْ ع َّي ْزَّةُّ وَّ إ َّق َّامَّ س َّاهِذَّ ي ْن ِ وَّ إ َّق َّامَّ إل َّذ ِى  ق ِى  ي َّذِةِ س َّاهِذَّي ْن ِ وَّ إسْي َّوَّى 
إلس  ُّهُّوذُّ ق ِى  إلْعَّذَّإلَّه ِ لَّكَّان َّ إلْحُّكْمُّ إ َّن ْ ي ُّح ْزَّح َّ إلس  َّى ْ ءُّ مِن ْ ي َّذَّى ْ مَّالِكِهِ إ ِلَّى إلْمُّذ َّعِى  لِأ َّن  َّ إلْن َّي ِّن َّه َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ ق َّا ِن ْ لَّمْ ي َّكُّن ِ إلس  َّى ْ ءُّ ق ِى  ي َّذَّى ْ 
إ َّجَّذٍ وَّ إذ َّعَّى ق ِي هِ إلْح َّصْمَّان ِ ج َّمِي عاً ق َّكُّل ُّ مَّن ْ إ َّق َّامَّ إلْن َّي ِّن َّه َّ ق َّهُّوَّ إ َّجَّق  ُّ ن ِهِ ق َّا ِن ْ إ َّق َّامَّ كُّل ُّ وَّإجِ ذٍ مِن ْهُّمَّا إلْن َّي ِّ ن َّه َّ ق َّا ِن  َّ إ َّجَّق  َّ إلْمُّذ َّعِي َّي ْن ِ مَّن ْ  
عُّذِّلَّ س َّاهِذَّإةُّ ق َّا ِن ِ إسْي َّوَّى إلس  ُّهُّوذُّ ق ِى  إلْعَّذَّإلَّه ِ ق َّا َّكْي َّزُّهُّمَّا س ُّهُّوذإً ي َّحْلِف ُّ ن ِالل َّهِ وَّ ي ُّذْق َّغُّ إ ِلَّي ْ هِ إلس  َّى ْ ءُّ هَّكَّذ َّإ ذ َّكَّزَّةُّ إ َّن ِى  رَّص ِى َّ إلل َّهُّ عَّن ْهُّ  
ق ِى  رِسَّالَّي ِهِ إ ِلَّى  َّ.
hadiths.en:
It is narrated by Shu'ayb from Abu Basir,  from Abu Abdullah  Imam Jafar ibn 
Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s}, that he mentioned: A group of people came to Commander 
of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s}, disputing ownership of a mule. Both parties 
presented evidence.  
One group claimed that they had raised it at their feeding trough, had neither sold it 
nor gifted it, and provided witnesses to support their claim.  
The other group also claimed that they had raised it at their feeding trough, had neither 
sold it nor gifted it, and provided their own witnesses.  
Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} ruled in favor of the group that had a greater number of 
witnesses and required them to take an oath to support their claim.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.64 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 27, H. 3344
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE JUDGMENT REGARDING TWO
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON THE JUDGMENT REGARDING TWO
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٧، ح ٣٣٤٤

hadiths.chapter: 27. CHAPTER ON THE JUDGMENT REGARDING TWO CLAIMANTS EACH PRESENTING EVIDENCE THAT THE RIGHT
hadiths.en:
Abu Basir narrated that he asked Abu Abdullah {a.s}, about a man who comes 
to a group of people and claims ownership of a house in their possession. He provides 
evidence, and the one in possession of the house also provides evidence that he 
inherited it from his father but does not know its exact history.  
Abu Abdullah {a.s}, replied: “The one with more evidence is required to take an oath, and 
the house is given to him." 
[AL SADUQ] 
The compiler of this book, may Allah {SWT} have mercy on him, commented: If the one in 
possession of the house claims it as his property and provides evidence, and the 
claimant also provides evidence for his claim, the correct ruling is that the house 
should be awarded to the claimant. This is bec ause Allah {SWT}, the Exalted, has made 
it obligatory for the claimant to produce evidence, not for the one in possession.  
However, in this case, the possessor stated that he inherited the house from his father 
and is unaware of its exact legal status. Therefore, the ruling requires the one with 
stronger evidence to take an oath and have the property awarded to him.  
If a person claims real estate, an animal, or other property, and provides two 
witnesses, and the possessor also provides two witnesses of equal credibility, the 
ruling is that the property should be transferred from the possessor to the claimant 
because the evidence rests upon the claimant. 
If the property is not in the possession of either party and both claim it, then the one 
who presents evidence is entitled to it. If both present evidence, the one whose 
witnesses are deemed more credible is entitled to it. If the witnesses are equally 
credible, then the one with more witnesses must take an oath, and the property is 
awarded to him. 
This explanation was mentioned by my father, may Allah {SWT} be pleased with him, in 
his letter to me. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.65

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 27, H. 3345
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE JUDGMENT REGARDING TWO CLAIMANTS EACH PRESENTING EVIDENCE THAT THE RIGHT

hadiths.chapter: 29. CHAPTER ON TESTIMONY AGAINST A WOMAN
hadiths.ar:
3347  -  وَّ كَّي َّث َّ مُّحَّم َّذُّ ن ْن ُّ إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ إَّلص َّق  َّارُّ رَّص ِى َّ إَّلل َّهُّ عَّن ْهُّ إ ِلَّى إ َّن ِى مُّحَّم َّذٍ إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ ن ْن ِ عَّلِى ٍّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : ق ِى  رَّج ُّلٍ  
إ َّرَّإذَّ إ َّن ْ ي َّس ْهَّذَّ عَّلَّى إِمْزَّإ َّة ٍ لَّي ْسَّ لَّهَّا ن ِمَّحْزَّمٍ هَّلْ ي َّح ُّور ُّ لَّهُّ إ َّن ْ ي َّس ْهَّذَّ عَّلَّي ْهَّا مِن ْ وَّرَّإءِ إَّلسِّي ْ زِ وَّ ي َّسْمَّغَّ كَّلاَّمَّهَّا إ ِذ َّإ س َّهِذَّ  
عَّذْلأَّن ِ إ َّن  َّهَّا ق ُّلاَّ ن َّه ُّ ن ِن ْث ُّ ق ُّلاَّن ٍ إَّل َّي ِى  ي ُّس ْهِذُّك َّ وَّ هَّذ َّإ كَّلاَّمُّهَّا إ َّوْ لأَّ ي َّح ُّور ُّ إَّلس  َّهَّاذَّة ُّ عَّلَّي ْهَّا جَّي  َّى ي َّن ْزُّر َّ وَّ ي ُّي ْ ن ِي َّهَّا ن ِعَّي ْن ِهَّا ق َّوَّق  َّغَّ 
عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم  
وَّ هَّذ َّإ إلي  َّوْق ِي غُّ عِن ْذِى  ن ِح َّطِّهِ ع.
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Ali ibn Yaqtin, from Abu al-Hasan al-Awwal {a.s}, who said: 
"There is no issue with bearing witness to a woman's acknowledgment (confession) 
without her unveiling her face, as long as she is identified either by her specific 
features or in the presence of someone who recognizes her."  
[AL SADUQ] 
However, in their view, it is not permissible  for witnesses to testify regarding her 
acknowledgment without her unveiling so that they may see her directly. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.67 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 29, H. 3346
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON TESTIMONY AGAINST A WOMAN
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON TESTIMONY AGAINST A WOMAN

hadiths.chapter: 29. CHAPTER ON TESTIMONY AGAINST A WOMAN
hadiths.en:
It is narrated that Muhammad ibn al -Hasan al -Saffar (may Allah {SWT} be 
pleased with him) wrote to Abu Muhammad al-Hasan ibn Ali {a.s} regarding a man who 
wanted to testify about a woman who was not his mahram (unmarriageable relative). 
He asked: "Is it permissible for him to testify about her from behind a curtain while 
hearing her speech, provided that two just witnesses testify that she is indeed so-and-
so, the daughter of so-and-so, who is calling you to bear witness and this is her voice? 
Or is it not permissible to testify about her until she uncovers her face and is identified 
by her appearance?" 
The Imam {a.s} replied: "She should veil her face partially (wear a niqab)  and appear 
before the witnesses, if Allah {SWT} wills." 
[AL SADUQ] 
This reply (tawqi’) was documented in his handwriting {a.s}. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.67 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.6 p.255 • Al-Istibsar, Vol.3 
p.19 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.27 p.401

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 29, H. 3347
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON TESTIMONY AGAINST A WOMAN
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON TESTIMONY AGAINST A WOMAN

hadiths.chapter: 30. CHAPTER ON INVALIDATING TESTIMONY REGARDING
hadiths.ar:
3349  -  وَّ ق ِى  رِوَّإي َّه ِ عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن ِ مَّي ْمُّون ٍ عَّن ِ إَّلص َّاذِق ِ ج َّعْق َّزِ ن ْن ِ مُّحَّم َّذٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِي هِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال  
إَّلْأ َّن ْصَّارِ إ ِلَّى إَّلن  َّن ِى ِّ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ ق َّق َّالَّ ي َّا رَّسُّولَّ إَّلل َّهِ إ ُّجِث  ُّ إ َّن ْ ي َّس ْهَّذَّ لِى  عَّلَّ ى ن َّح ْ لٍ ن َّحَّلْي ُّهَّا إِن ْن ِى  ق َّال 
وَّلَّذ  سِوَّإة ن َّس ْهَّذُّ عَّلَّى إَّلْح َّن َّف ِ
hadiths.en:
It is narrated by Isma'il ibn Muslim from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq 
{a.s} from his father Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} that he said: 
"Testimonies are invalidated in cases of usury (riba) and bias (janaf). If the witnesses 
say, 'We do not know,' then release them. However, if they are found to have known, 
then they should be disciplined (ta'zir)." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.68 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 30, H. 3348
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON INVALIDATING TESTIMONY REGARDING
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON INVALIDATING TESTIMONY REGARDING
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٠، ح ٣٣٤٨

hadiths.chapter: 30. CHAPTER ON INVALIDATING TESTIMONY REGARDING
hadiths.en:
In a narration from Abdullah ibn Maymun  from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad 
Al-Sadiq {a.s} from his father Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s}, it is stated: 
"A man from the Ansar came to the Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) and 
said:  
‘O Messenger of Allah {SWT}, I wish for you to bear witness for me regarding a palm grove 
that I have gifted to my son.'  
The Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) asked: ‘Do you have any children 
besides him?' He replied: ‘Yes.'  
The Prophet {saws} then asked: ‘Did you gift them as you gifted him?'  
He said: ‘No.'  
The Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) said: ‘Indeed, we, the community of 
Prophets, do not bear witness to bias (janaf).'" 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.69 • Al-Wafi, Vol.16 p.1039 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’ah, Vol.27 
p.414

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 30, H. 3349
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON INVALIDATING TESTIMONY REGARDING
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON INVALIDATING TESTIMONY REGARDING
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٠، ح ٣٣٤٩

hadiths.chapter: 30. CHAPTER ON INVALIDATING TESTIMONY REGARDING INJUSTICE, USURY, AND CONTRADICTION TO THE
hadiths.en:
In a narration from Abu al-Husayn Muhammad ibn Ja'far al -Asadi (may Allah 
{SWT} be pleased with him), Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said: 
"Do not bear witness for one who divorces contrary to the Sunnah."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.69 • Al-Wafi, Vol.16 p.1040 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’ah, Vol.27 
p.414

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 30, H. 3350

hadiths.chapter: 31. CHAPTER ON TESTIMONY BASED ON TESTIMONY
hadiths.ar:
3352  -  وَّ رَّوَّى ع ِي َّاب ُّ ن ْن ُّ إ ِن ْزَّإهِي مَّ عَّن ْ ج َّعْق َّزِ ن ْن ِ مُّحَّم َّذٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِي هِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم 
س َّهَّاذَّة َّ رَّج ُّلٍ عَّلَّى س َّهَّاذَّة ِ رَّج ُّلٍ إ ِلأ َّ س َّهَّاذَّة َّ رَّج ُّلَّي ْن ِ عَّلَّى س َّهَّاذَّة ِ رَّج ُّلٍ
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"If a man testifies based on the testimony of another man, his testimony is accepted, 
and it counts as half a testimony. However, if two just men testify based on the 
testimony of one man, it is considered equivalent to the testimony of one man."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.69 • Al-Wafi, Vol.16 p.1017 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’ah, Vol.27 
p.404 • Mustadrak Al-Wasa’il, Vol.17 p.442 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 31, H. 3351
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON TESTIMONY BASED ON TESTIMONY
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON TESTIMONY BASED ON TESTIMONY
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣١، ح ٣٣٥١

hadiths.chapter: 31. CHAPTER ON TESTIMONY BASED ON TESTIMONY
hadiths.ar:
3353   -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن ِ سِن َّان ٍ عَّن ْ عَّن ْذِ إَّلز َّجْمَّن ِ ن ْن ِ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّ مُّ : ق ِى  رَّج ُّلٍ  
س َّهِذَّ عَّلَّى س َّهَّاذَّة ِ رَّج ُّلٍ ق َّح َّاءَّ إَّلز َّج ُّلُّ ق َّق َّالَّ إ ِن ِّى  لَّمْ إ ُّس ْهِذْةُّ ق َّال مَّا وَّ إ ِن ْ كَّان َّث ْ عَّذَّإلَّي ُّهُّمَّا وَّإجِذَّة ً 
لَّمْ ي َّح ُّز ْ س َّهَّاذَّي ُّه
hadiths.en:
Ghiyath ibn Ibrahim narrated from Imam Jafar Al -Sadiq {a.s} from his father 
Imam Al-Baqir {a.s}: "Indeed, Imam Ali {a.s} would not validate the testimony of one man 
based on the testimony of another man, except if there were two men testifying based 
on testimony of one man." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.70 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.6 p.255 • Al-Istibsar, Vol.3 
p.21 • Al-Wafi, Vol.16 p.1017 • Al-Wafi, Vol.16 p.1017 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.27 p.403 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, 
Vol.27 p.403 • Al-Fusul Al-Muhimmah, Vol.2 p.509 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 31, H. 3352
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON TESTIMONY BASED ON TESTIMONY
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON TESTIMONY BASED ON TESTIMONY
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣١، ح ٣٣٥٢

hadiths.chapter: 31. CHAPTER ON TESTIMONY BASED ON TESTIMONY
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan, from Abdurrahman ibn Abi Abdullah,  
from Abu Abdullah  Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} regarding a man who 
testified based on the testimony of another man, and then the original man said:  
‘I did not make him a witness,'  
Imam {a.s} said: "The testimony of the more upright of the two is accepted, but if their 
uprightness is equal, then his testimony is not accepted."  
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.399 • Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.399 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.70 • Tahdhib 
Al-Ahkam, Vol.6 p.256 • Awali Al-La’ali, Vol.3 p.543 • Al-Wafi, Vol.16 p.1018 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.27 p.405 
• Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.27 p.405 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 31, H. 3353
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON TESTIMONY BASED ON TESTIMONY
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON TESTIMONY BASED ON TESTIMONY
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣١، ح ٣٣٥٣

hadiths.chapter: 31. CHAPTER ON TESTIMONY BASED ON TESTIMONY
hadiths.ar:
3355  -  ورَّوَّى إَّلْعَّلاَّءُّ عَّن ْ مُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ مُّسْلِمٍ ق َّالَّ : سَّا َّلْث ُّ إ َّن َّا ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ِ إَّلذ ِّمِّى ِّ وَّ إَّلْ عَّن ْذِ ي ُّس ْهَّذَّإن ِ عَّلَّى  
س َّهَّاذَّة ٍ ي ُّم َّ ي ُّسْلِمُّ إَّلذ ِّمِّى  ُّ وَّ ي ُّعْي َّق ُّ إَّلْعَّن ْذُّ إ َّ ي َّح ُّور ُّ س َّهَّاذَّي ُّهُّمَّا عَّلَّى مَّا كَّان َّا إ ُّ س ْهِذَّإ عَّلَّي ْهِ ق َّال 
ج َّي ْز  ج َّار َّب ْ س َّهَّاذَّي ُّهُّمَّا
hadiths.en:
Safwan ibn Yahya asked Abu al -Hasan {a.s} about a man who made his 
employee a witness to a testimony and then separated from him, would his testimony 
be valid after their separation?  
Imam {a.s} said: "Yes." 
He further asked: What if a Jew was made a witness to a testimony and then embraced 
Islam, would his testimony be valid?  
Imam {a.s} said: "Yes." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.70 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.27 p.387 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 31, H. 3354
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON TESTIMONY BASED ON TESTIMONY
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON TESTIMONY BASED ON TESTIMONY
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣١، ح ٣٣٥٤

hadiths.chapter: 31. CHAPTER ON TESTIMONY BASED ON TESTIMONY
hadiths.ar:
3356  -  وَّ رَّوَّى ع ِي َّاب ُّ ن ْن ُّ إ ِن ْزَّإهِي مَّ عَّن ْ ج َّعْق َّزِ ن ْن ِ مُّحَّم َّذٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِي هِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ ق َّالَّ عَّلِى    عَّلَّي ْهِ  إَّلس َّلاَّم 
ي َّح ُّور ُّ س َّهَّاذَّة   عَّلَّى س َّهَّاذَّة ٍ ق ِى  جَّذٍّ وَّ لأَّ كَّق َّالَّه   ق ِى  جَّذ
hadiths.en:
Al-Ala narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim:  
He said: I asked Abu Ja’far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} about a dhimmi (non-
Muslim under Islamic protection) and a slave who bore witness to a testimony. Then 
the dhimmi embraced Islam and the slave was emancipated, would their testimony 
about what they were made witnesses to before be valid?  
Imam {a.s} said: "Yes, if goodness is known from them afterward, the testimony is valid." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, V.3 p.70 • Al-Wafi, Vol.16 p.980 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.27 p.387 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 31, H. 3355
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON TESTIMONY BASED ON TESTIMONY
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON TESTIMONY BASED ON TESTIMONY
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣١، ح ٣٣٥٥

hadiths.chapter: 31. CHAPTER ON TESTIMONY BASED ON TESTIMONY
hadiths.ar:
3357   -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ مُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ مُّسْلِمٍ عَّن ِ إَّلْن َّاق ِزِ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : ق ِى  إَّلس  َّهَّاذَّة ِ عَّلَّى س َّهَّاذ ة ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ وَّ هُّوَّ 
ن ِالْحَّص ْزَّة ِ ق ِى   إَّلْن َّلَّذِ ق َّال  ي َّمْن َّعُّهُّ مِن ْ إ َّن ْ 
ي َّحْص ُّزَّ وَّ ي ُّق ِي مَّهَّا ق َّلاَّ ن َّا ْسَّ ن ِا ِق َّامَّه ِ إَّلس  َّهَّاذَّة ِ عَّلَّى س َّهَّاذَّي ِهِ
hadiths.en:
Ghiyath ibn Ibrahim narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} 
from his father Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s}: 
Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} said: "Testimony upon testimony is not valid in cases 
involving prescribed punishments (hudud), nor is sponsorship (kafalah) valid in cases 
involving prescribed punishments." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.70 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol.6 p.256 • Al -Wafi, Vol.16 
p.1019 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.27 p.404  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 31, H. 3356
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON TESTIMONY BASED ON TESTIMONY
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON TESTIMONY BASED ON TESTIMONY
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣١، ح ٣٣٥٦

hadiths.chapter: 31. CHAPTER ON TESTIMONY BASED ON TESTIMONY
hadiths.ar:
3358  -  وَّ رَّوَّى عَّمْزُّو ن ْن ُّ ج ُّمَّي ْغٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِي هِ عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال «
ق َّالُّوإ إ َّصْلَّحَّك َّ إَّلل َّهُّ كَّي ْف َّ ي َّز ِي ذُّ وَّ ي َّن ْق ُّضُّ ق َّال  
وَّ لَّأ ي َّح ُّور ُّ س َّهَّاذَّة   عَّلَّى س َّهَّاذَّة ٍ عَّلَّى س َّهَّاذَّة ٍ.
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from Al-Baqir, Abu Ja’far Imam Muhammad 
ibn Ali {a.s} regarding testimony upon the testimony of a man who is present in the town. 
Imam {a.s} said: "Yes, even if he is behind a column. It is permissible if he is unable to 
present it himself due to some excuse preventing him from attending and presenting 
it. In such a case, there is no harm in establishing testimony based on his testimony."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.71 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.6 p.256 • Al-Istibsar, Vol.3 
p.20 • Al-Wafi, Vol.16 p.1020 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.27 p.402 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 31, H. 3357
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON TESTIMONY BASED ON TESTIMONY
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON TESTIMONY BASED ON TESTIMONY
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣١، ح ٣٣٥٧

hadiths.chapter: 31. CHAPTER ON TESTIMONY BASED ON TESTIMONY
hadiths.en:
Amr ibn Jumay' narrated from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -
Sadiq {a.s} from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s}: 
Imam {a.s} said: "Let someone trustworthy bear witness to your testimony."  
They asked: ‘May Allah {SWT} keep you well, how can it increase or decrease?'  
Imam {a.s} replied: “No, but it should be someone who preserves it for you."  
Imam {a.s} also said: "It is not permissible to testify upon a testimony that is based on 
another testimony." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.71 • Al-Wafi, Vol.16 p.1020 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’ah, Vol.27 
p.404

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 31, H. 3358

reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON TESTIMONY BASED ON TESTIMONY
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣١، ح ٣٣٥٨

hadiths.chapter: 32. CHAPTER ON PRECAUTION IN GIVING TESTIMONY
hadiths.ar:
3360   -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ عَّلِى ِّ ن ْن ِ سُّوَّي ْذٍ ق َّالَّ : ق ُّلْث ُّ لِأ َّن ِى  إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ إَّلْمَّاص ِى  عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ي ُّس ْهِذُّن ِى  هَّو ُّلأَّءِ عَّلَّى إ ِج ْوَّإن ِ ى  
ق َّال  
ق َّالَّ مُّصَّن ِّف ُّ هَّذ َّإ إلْكِي َّاب ِ رَّجِمَّهُّ إلل َّهُّ هَّكَّذ َّإ وَّج َّذْي ُّهُّ ق ِى  ن ُّسْح َّي ِى  وَّ وَّج َّذْب ُّ ق ِى  ع َّي ْزِ ن ُّسْح َّي ِى   وَّ إ ِن ْ ج ِق ْث َّ عَّلَّى إ َّج ِي ك َّ ص َّزَّرإً ق َّلَّا.وَّ 
مَّعْن َّاهُّمَّا ق َّزِي ث   وَّ ذ َّلِك َّ إ َّن  َّهُّ إ ِذ َّإ كَّان َّ لِكَّاق ِزٍ عَّلَّى مُّو ْمِن ٍ جَّق    وَّ هُّوَّ مُّوسِز  مَّلِى    ن ِهِ وَّج َّث َّ إ ِق َّامَّ ه ُّ إلس  َّهَّاذَّة ِ عَّلَّي ْهِ ن ِذ َّلِك َّ وَّ إ ِن ْ كَّان َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ 
ص َّزَّر  ن ِن َّق ْضٍ مِن ْ مَّالِهِ  وَّ مَّي َّى كَّان َّ إلْمُّو ْمِن ُّ مُّعْسِزإً وَّ عَّلِمَّ إلس  َّاهِذُّ ن ِذ َّلِك َّ ق َّلَّا ي َّحِل ُّ لَّهُّ إ ِق َّامَّه ُّ إلس  َّهَّاذَّة ِ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ ِ ذْج َّالُّ إلص  َّزَّ رِ عَّلَّي ْهِ  
ن ِا َّن ْ ي ُّحْن َّسَّ إ َّوْ ي ُّح ْزَّح َّ عَّن ْ مَّسْق َّظِ رَّإ ْسِهِ إ َّوْ ي ُّح ْزَّح َّ ج َّاذِمُّهُّ عَّن ْ مِلْكِهِ وَّ هَّكَّذ َّإ لَّأ ي َّح ُّور ُّ لِلْمُّو ْمِن ِ  إ َّن ْ ي ُّق ِي مَّ س َّهَّاذَّة ً ي ُّق ْي َّلُّ ن ِهَّا مُّو ْمِن   
ن ِكَّاق ِزٍ وَّ مَّي َّى كَّان َّ ع َّي ْزُّ ذ َّلِك َّ ق َّي َّح ِث ُّ إ ِق َّامَّي ُّهَّا عَّلَّي ْهِ  ق َّا ِن  َّ ق ِى  صِق َّاب ِ إلْمُّو ْمِن ِ إ َّل َّأ ي ُّحَّذِّب َّ إ َّمَّان َّي َّهُّ إلْأ َّصْذِق َّاءَّ وَّ لَّأ ي َّكْي ُّمَّ س َّهَّاذَّة َّ إلْأ َّعْذَّإءِ.
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Ali ibn Ghurab, from Abu Abdillah {a.s}:  
"Do not testify to a testimony until you know it as clearly as you know your own palm." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.71 • Al-Fusul Al-Muhimmah, Vol.2 p.507 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 32, H. 3359
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON PRECAUTION IN GIVING TESTIMONY
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON PRECAUTION IN GIVING TESTIMONY
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٢، ح ٣٣٥٩

hadiths.chapter: 32. CHAPTER ON PRECAUTION IN GIVING TESTIMONY
hadiths.ar:
3361  -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ عُّمَّزَّ ن ْن ِ ي َّز ِي ذَّ ق َّالَّ : ق ُّلْث ُّ لِأ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ رَّج ُّل  ي ُّس ْهِذُّن ِى  عَّلَّى إَّلس هَّاذَّة ِ ق َّا َّعْزِف ُّ  
ج َّطِّى  وَّ ج َّاي َّمِى  وَّ لأَّ إ َّذ ْكُّزُّ مِن َّ إَّلْن َّاق ِى  ق َّلِي لاً وَّ لأَّ كَّي ِي زإً ق َّق َّال ق َّه ً وَّ مَّعَّك َّ رَّج ُّل  ي ِق َّه   ق َّاس ْهَّذْ لَّه  
وَّ رُّوِى
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Ali ibn Suwayd: “I said to Abu al-Hasan al-Madhi {a.s} (Imam 
Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim {a.s}): "These people call me to testify against my brothers."  
Imam {a.s} said: 'Yes, establish the testimony for them, even if you fear harm for your 
brother.'" 
[AL SADUQ] 
The compiler of this book said: This is how I found it in my copy, and in another copy, I 
found it stated: 'If you fear harm for your brother, then do not testify.' Both meanings 
are close in interpretation. 
The explanation is that if a disbeliever has a right over a believer and the believer is 
wealthy and capable of fulfilling it, then it is obligatory to establish the testimony 
against him, even if it causes financial loss. However, if the believer is in fi nancial 
distress and the witness knows this, then it is not permissible for him to establish the 
testimony that could harm the believer by leading to imprisonment, displacement 
from his home, or the loss of his servant. 
Likewise, it is not permissible for a believer to provide testimony that would lead to the 
killing of a believer in favor of a disbeliever. However, if the situation is different, then 
the testimony must be established because one attributes of a believer is that he does 
not betray his trust, whether among friends or by concealing testimony from enemies. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.72 • Al-Wafi, Vol.16 p.1026 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 32, H. 3360

hadiths.chapter: 32. CHAPTER ON PRECAUTION IN GIVING TESTIMONY
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Umar ibn Yazid, who said: 
I said to Abu Abdullah {a.s}: "A man calls me to testify, and I recognize my handwriting 
and my seal, but I do not remember anything else, whether little or much."  
Imam {a.s} said: 'If your companion is trustworthy and you have another trustworthy 
man with you, then testify for him.'" 
[AL SADUQ] 
It is also narrated that: "Testimony cannot be established except with knowledge. 
Whoever wishes may write a document or engrave a seal."  
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.382 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.72 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.6 p.258 
• Al-Istibsar, Vol.3 p.22 • Awali Al -La’ali, Vol.3 p.542 • Al -Wafi, Vol.16 p.1031 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’ah, Vol.27 
p.321 • Al-Fusul Al-Muhimmah, Vol.1 p.585

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 32, H. 3361

hadiths.chapter: 33. CHAPTER ON THE TESTIMONY OF THE EXECUTOR FOR
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn al-Hasan al-Saffar, may Allah {SWT} have mercy on him, wrote 
to Abu Muhammad al-Hasan ibn Ali {a.s}: 
"Is the testimony of the executor (al -wasi) for the deceased regarding a debt owed to 
him by a man acceptable along with another just witness?"  
Imam {a.s} replied: "If another just witness testifies with him, then the claimant must 
take an oath." 
He also wrote to Imam {a.s}: "Is it permissible for the executor to testify for the heir of 
the deceased, whether young or old, regarding a right owed to him by the deceased or 
by another person, especially if he is the custodian of the young heir but not the 
custodian of the older one?"  
Imam {a.s} replied: "Yes, the executor should testify truthfully and must not conceal his 
testimony." 
Furthermore, he wrote to Imam {a.s}: "Is the testimony of the executor against the 
deceased regarding a debt, along with another just witness, acceptable?"  
Imam {a.s} replied: "Yes, but only after an oath." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.394 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.73 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.6 p.247 
• Al-Wafi, Vol.16 p.989 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.27 p.371

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 33, H. 3362
